name: Panzanella Italian Bread Salad- Cooking Live
recipeIngredient: 1 large round loaf peasant bread, cut into 1-inch cubes, about 4 cups
1 clove garlic, smashed to a paste
salt and freshly ground black pepper
5 ripe plum tomatoes, seeded and chopped
1 green bell pepper, seeded and chopped
1 yellow, orange or red bell pepper, seeded and chopped
1 medium bulb fennel, trimmed and chopped with the feathery greens (fronds)
1/2 cup nicoise olives, pitted and halved
recipeInstructions:
1. Spread the bread cubes out on a baking sheet and leave uncovered to dry for 24 hours. Alternatively, bake the cubes in an oven preheated to 300°F for 10 to 20 minutes until dried but not toasted.
2. In a large bowl whisk together the shallots, garlic, lemon rind, and balsamic vinegar. Season with salt and pepper. Gradually add the olive oil while whisking constantly until well combined.
3. Add the tomatoes, bell peppers, fennel, and olives to the bowl; toss to combine. Adjust seasoning with additional salt and pepper.
4. Thirty minutes before serving, add the reserved fennel greens, basil leaves shredded, and bread cubes to the salad; toss to coat. Let the salad stand at room temperature for 30 minutes.
Yield: 8 servings
